items.text:
Notice: Launch of CampusConnect App

Date: April 15, 9:00 AM
Location: Student Union, Room 210

The Office of Student Affairs is pleased to announce the launch of CampusConnect, a new mobile application designed to help students access services, view schedules, and receive real-time alerts about campus events. A demonstration session will be held in Room 210 on April 15 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, hosted by Ms. Patel from the Information Technology Office.

Key features include:

- Personalized class and exam reminders
- Direct messaging with academic advisors
- Live dining hall menu and shuttle tracking
- Secure access to digital ID and library account

A $5 fee will be charged once per academic year to cover server maintenance, automatically billed through the student portal. Students who attend the launch session will receive a complimentary launch month at no charge. Please bring a charged smartphone and your student ID. For questions, contact campusconnect@university.edu.

items.text:
Notice: E-Waste Recycling Drive

Date: May 22-24, 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
Location: Parking Lot D, Engineering Quad

The Office of Sustainability will host a three-day electronic waste recycling drive to help students, faculty, and staff dispose of unwanted electronics safely. All collected items will be sorted by certified handlers and recycled in accordance with state regulations.

Accepted items:

- Laptops and tablets
- Mobile phones and chargers
- Printers and small appliances
- Cables, batteries, and computer mice

Not accepted:

- Large household appliances such as refrigerators
- Televisions over forty inches
- Items containing liquid or hazardous chemicals

Participants must remove personal data before drop-off; the office does not provide data wiping services. A free reusable tote will be given to the first 100 participants each day. Volunteers from the Engineering Sustainability Club will assist with sorting. Questions may be directed to ewaste@university.edu.

options.B: A forty-five-inch television from a dorm room

options.D: A small kitchen appliance no longer in use

questions.explanation: The notice excludes televisions over forty inches; a forty-five-inch television would not be accepted.

items.text:
Introduction to Biology — Lab Component Guidelines (Spring Semester)

The laboratory portion of BIOL 101 contributes 35% of the final course grade and is required for all enrolled students. Weekly lab reports account for 20%, two practical exams contribute another 10%, and lab attendance and preparation make up the remaining 5%.

All lab reports must be submitted through the online portal by 11:59 PM on the Sunday following each scheduled lab. Late reports are accepted within 48 hours of the deadline for a 15% penalty. Reports submitted after 48 hours will not receive credit.

A non-refundable safety equipment fee of $25 must be paid by January 22 at the Bursar's Office in Anderson Hall. The fee covers safety goggles, lab notebooks, and access to the microscope library. Students who miss more than three lab sessions without an excused absence will be required to repeat the course.

Please contact your TA, Ms. Lin, in Room 204 during her Tuesday office hours (2:00-4:00 PM) for any questions about grading or makeup labs.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what percentage of the course grade comes from lab attendance and preparation?
questions.explanation: The syllabus states that attendance and preparation make up the remaining 5%.

options.A: Submit the first weekly lab report
options.B: Pay the $25 non-refundable safety equipment fee
options.C: Reserve a microscope at Anderson Hall
options.D: Register for a makeup lab with the TA

questions.explanation: The syllabus says the $25 safety fee must be paid by January 22 at the Bursar's Office.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who submits a lab report 60 hours after the deadline?

options.A: The student will be charged an additional safety fee.
options.B: The student will not receive any credit for that report.
options.C: The student will lose only 15% of the report's value.
options.D: The student will need to repeat the entire course.

questions.explanation: Reports submitted more than 48 hours after the deadline will not receive credit.

items.text:
Wi-Fi Security Certificate Update — March 18

The campus wireless network certificate will be updated on March 18 between 6:00 AM and 8:00 AM. After the update, all devices currently connected to CampusNet must reconfigure their secure connection before regaining access.

Details of the update:

- Affected service: CampusNet secure wireless
- Outage window: 6:00 AM - 8:00 AM, March 18
- Devices impacted: laptops, smartphones, tablets, and printers
- Drop-in support: Library Help Desk, 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, March 18-19
- Self-service guide: available on the IT portal

A $10 device-setup support fee will be charged for technicians making in-room visits to faculty offices, though walk-in help at the Library Help Desk remains free for all students. Devices that fail to update after March 20 may be temporarily blocked from network access.

Questions may be directed to the Network Operations Center at netops@example.edu or to Mr. Kowalski at extension 4582. Students are encouraged to update before the weekend to avoid service interruptions during midterm week.

items.text:
Updated Printing Quota and Kiosk Notice

Effective Monday, June 15, the library printing system will operate under a new quota and station layout. All current student accounts will be reset to the new monthly limit, and several kiosks will be relocated during the transition week.

Key changes:
- Each undergraduate account receives 200 free pages per month.
- Pages beyond the quota are billed at $0.08 per black-and-white page and $0.25 per color page.
- Unused pages no longer roll over to the next month.
- The third-floor kiosk has been moved to the second-floor reference area near the help desk.
- The basement kiosk will be closed permanently after June 30.

Students who arrive before 9:00 AM may use any open kiosk without a wait. Please load files through the new portal at print.collegeport.edu. The IT help desk in Carver Hall, Room 118, can assist with login problems Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Contact Mr. Howard at extension 3201 for billing questions.

items.text:
Subject: Summer Research Stipend Application — Important Updates

Dear Students,

This email outlines the updated procedures for the Summer Research Stipend application, due May 30. Please read carefully, as several requirements have changed since last year.

To apply, you must:
- Submit your faculty endorsement form by May 15 through the portal at research.collegeport.edu
- Upload a two-page project proposal in PDF format
- Provide an official transcript showing a minimum 3.2 GPA
- Pay the $25.00 administrative processing fee

Late applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. Selected students will receive a $4,500 stipend distributed in two payments: $2,000 on June 15 and $2,500 on July 30. Recipients must attend the orientation meeting in Sutton Hall, Room 312, on June 5 at 2:00 PM.

If you have questions about eligibility or proposal formatting, please email researchoffice@college.edu or visit our office in Hopkins Building, Room 204, Monday through Thursday from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. We're encouraging all eligible juniors to apply.

Best regards,
Dr. Allen Chen
Office of Undergraduate Research

items.text:
Subject: Saturday Urban Sketching Walk — Bring Your Camera or Pad

Hi Sketchers,

The Visual Arts Club is hosting our spring urban sketching and photography walk on Saturday, May 18, from 10:00 AM to 1:30 PM. We'll meet at the bench outside Carlson Hall and then walk together through the historic district, stopping at three locations chosen by club leaders.

The walk is open to all students, regardless of experience. Please bring either a sketchpad and pencils or a camera; we'll have a small loan kit at the meeting point if you need supplies. The optional materials kit is $4 and includes paper, two pencils, and an eraser.

Light refreshments will be served during the lunch stop at the public square. If you plan to attend, please reply by Thursday, May 16 so we can confirm numbers with the cafe.

Thanks,
Dana Park
Visual Arts Club

items.text:
NOTICE: Updated Recreation Center Membership Policy

Beginning Monday, August 26, the campus Recreation Center will update its membership policy. The following rules will apply to all student members:

- A current student ID must be presented at every visit. Replacement IDs cost $8 at the Card Office in Reynolds Hall.
- Equipment lockers may be reserved for a full semester at a $15 fee, payable online through the recreation portal.
- Guests must be signed in by a student member at the front desk. Each member may host one guest per visit, and the guest pass is $5.
- The pool and indoor track will close one hour earlier, at 9:00 PM, on weekdays during the fall semester.

Members with questions should contact Coach Ramirez at the front desk or by email during posted office hours, Monday through Friday, 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM.

items.text:
Notice: Updated Allergen Policy for Dining Halls
Effective: October 16, 2023
Location: All Campus Dining Halls

The university has revised its allergen policy to enhance safety for students with food allergies. As of October 16, all dining halls will require students to declare any severe allergies through the online portal before the start of each semester. A $5.00 penalty will be applied to meal plans if a student fails to update their allergy information by the deadline.

Key updates:
- A dedicated allergen-free station will be available in each dining hall during lunch and dinner hours (11:00 AM to 8:00 PM).
- Students must present their student ID at the station to receive a meal.
- Menu items containing common allergens will be clearly labeled.
- Any student who experiences an allergic reaction should report it immediately to dining hall staff.

For questions or to update your allergy information, please contact the Dining Services Office at extension 555-0199.

items.text:
Notice: Change to Package Delivery Procedure
Effective: November 5, 2023
Location: Campus Mail Center, Room 101, Anderson Hall

Starting November 5, the Campus Mail Center will implement a new procedure for package delivery to improve security and efficiency. All packages must now be collected within 48 hours of notification, or a $5.00 late fee will be charged. Students must bring their student ID and the delivery notification email to receive their package.

Important changes:
- Packages can be picked up Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.
- After 48 hours, packages will be moved to the overflow storage area and a $5.00 fee will apply.
- Packages not collected within 7 days will be returned to the sender.
- For oversized packages, students must schedule a pickup appointment online.

For questions about this policy, contact the Mail Center Supervisor at extension 555-0142.

Subject: Flu Shot Appointment Confirmation

From: Health Services <healthservices@university.edu>
To: Maria Chen <maria.chen@university.edu>

Dear Maria,

This email confirms your appointment for a flu shot at the Student Health Clinic on November 10, 2023, at 10:30 AM. Please arrive 15 minutes early to complete the required paperwork. The appointment will take place in Room 204 of the Health Services Building.

Please bring your student ID and your insurance card. If you don't have insurance, you can still receive the shot for a $25.00 fee, payable at the time of service. We recommend wearing a short-sleeved shirt to make the vaccination easier.

If you need to reschedule, please contact us at least 24 hours in advance by calling extension 555-0211. We look forward to seeing you.

Best regards,
Dr. Patricia Lee
Director, Student Health Clinic

items.text:
Subject: Study Group for Biology 101 Final Exam

From: James Park <james.park@university.edu>
To: Biology 101 Students <bio101-students@university.edu>

Hi everyone,

I'm organizing a study group for the Biology 101 final exam on December 15. We'll meet in the library's Group Study Room 3 on December 10 and 12 from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM. Each session will focus on key topics from the second half of the semester, including cell division and genetics.

To join, please reply to this email by December 5. There's a $5.00 fee to cover printed materials and snacks, which you can pay at the first meeting. I'll send out a study guide to everyone who signs up. If you can't afford the fee, don't worry—just let me know, and we can work something out.

Also, please bring your textbook and any notes from class. We'll review practice questions together. Let me know if you have any questions!

Best,
James Park

items.text:
Campus Bicycle Rental Program – Spring Guide

Spring semester bicycle rentals are now available through the Campus Recreation Office. Rentals are offered on a first-come, first-served basis from March 1 to May 31. Bicycles can be picked up at the Bike Hub located behind the Health Center. A refundable deposit of $45 is required at the time of rental. Helmets are provided free of charge and must be worn at all times while riding. All bicycles must be returned by 6:00 PM on the final rental day. Late returns will incur a $10 fee per day. Bicycles are inspected before and after each rental. Riders are responsible for any damage beyond normal wear. The following rules apply:
- Bicycles must be locked when not in use.
- Riders must obey all campus traffic laws.
- Riding is permitted only on paved paths and roads.
- Bicycles must not be modified or repaired by renters.

For more information or to reserve a bicycle, visit the Campus Recreation Office in Room 112 or call (555) 987-6543.

items.text:
Campus Mailroom – Holiday Hours Change

The Campus Mailroom will adjust its hours of operation for the upcoming holiday break. Starting Friday, December 18, the mailroom will close at 2:00 PM instead of the usual 5:00 PM. From Monday, December 21 through Friday, January 1, the mailroom will be closed entirely. Normal hours will resume on Monday, January 4. All packages and letters must be picked up by 2:00 PM on December 18 to avoid being held over the break. Any mail left after this time will be stored securely but will not be accessible until the mailroom reopens. A $5 fee will be applied to any package that requires special handling during the closure. Students who expect to receive time-sensitive items are strongly encouraged to arrange for alternative delivery addresses. For urgent inquiries during the closure, contact Campus Security at (555) 555-0199. The mailroom is located in the Student Center, Room 101.

items.text:
Subject: Flu Shot Appointment Confirmation

Dear Alex,

This email confirms your appointment for a flu shot at the Campus Health Clinic on Friday, November 12, at 10:30 AM. Please arrive 10 minutes early to complete a brief health questionnaire. The appointment will take place in Room 302 of the Health Services Building. A valid student ID is required to check in. The flu shot is provided at no cost to all currently enrolled students. However, if you miss your appointment without canceling at least 24 hours in advance, a $15 missed-appointment fee will be charged to your student account. You can cancel or reschedule by logging into the Patient Portal or by calling the clinic at (555) 555-1234. Please bring your insurance card if you have one, though it is not required for this visit. The clinic is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Wear a short-sleeved shirt to allow easy access to your upper arm.

Best regards,

Dr. Maria Chen
Campus Health Clinic Director

Subject: Invitation to the Annual Cultural Festival

Dear students,

The International Student Association invites you to our Annual Cultural Festival on March 20, from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM in the Student Union Plaza. This event features food from 15 countries, live music, and dance performances.

Tickets are $5.00 in advance and $8.00 at the door. You can purchase them at the Student Activities Office, Room 101, until March 18. Volunteers who help set up or clean up will receive free entry and a T-shirt. Please sign up by March 10 if you are interested.

We hope to see you there!

Best regards,
Luis Ramirez
President, International Student Association

Subject: Your bookstore order is ready for pickup

Dear Maria,

Your textbook order (Order #BX-78432) is now available for pickup at the Campus Bookstore in the Student Union Building. Your order includes:
- Biology 101: Concepts and Connections (3rd Edition) — $85.00
- Lab Manual for Biology 101 — $25.00
- Chemistry 101: Principles and Reactions (2nd Edition) — $110.00

Pickup hours are Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, and Saturday, 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. The bookstore is closed on Sundays. Please bring your student ID and the order confirmation email. If someone else will pick up your order, they must bring a signed note from you along with their ID.

Orders not picked up within 10 business days will be returned to inventory and you'll receive a refund, minus a $5 restocking fee.

If you have any questions, reply to this email or call us at 555-0192.

Best regards,
Campus Bookstore

Subject: Study Group for Biology Exam

Hi everyone,

I'm organizing a study group for the upcoming BIOL 101 final exam on December 15. We'll meet in the library's Room 204 on the following Saturdays: November 10, November 17, and November 24, from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM. Each session will cover a specific topic: cell biology, genetics, and ecology. I've created a shared folder with practice questions and notes. Please bring your textbook and any questions you have.

To cover printing costs for handouts, I'm asking everyone to contribute $5.00. Please bring cash or use the online portal to pay by November 5. If you cannot afford it, let me know privately.

We still need a volunteer to lead the genetics session. If you're interested, reply to this email by November 3. Also, please confirm your attendance for the first session by November 1 so I can reserve the room.

Best regards,
Alex Chen

items.text:
Notice: Vending Machine Refund Procedure

Date: October 15, 2024

Due to a technical issue, some vending machines in Anderson Hall and the Student Union may have charged incorrect amounts. If you lost money in a machine, please follow this procedure:

- Locate the machine number (found on the front panel).
- Fill out a refund request form at the Campus Services office (Room 103, Student Union) or online at the university portal.
- Include your name, student ID, the machine number, the amount lost ($5.00 maximum per request), and the date of the transaction.
- Submit the form within 14 days of the transaction date.

Refunds will be processed within 10 business days and credited to your student account. For amounts over $5.00, please contact the vending company directly at support@vendingco.com. We apologize for the inconvenience.

Contact: Campus Services, (555) 123-4567, or visit Room 103 during office hours (9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, Monday to Friday).

Subject: Shift swap request for next Wednesday

Hi Jordan,

I'm writing to ask whether you would be willing to swap shifts with me at the campus bookstore. I'm scheduled to work the 2:00 to 8:00 p.m. shift on Wednesday, October 18, but I have a microbiology midterm at 6:00 p.m. the same evening that I just learned about. If you could take my Wednesday slot, I would gladly take your 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. shift this Saturday, October 21, in return.

If the swap works for you, please let Ms. Patel at the front desk know by Friday so she can update the schedule and process the $5 swap fee on my account. I would really appreciate it, and I'm happy to bring you coffee from the union as a thank-you next week.

Thanks so much,
David

items.text:
Notice: Library Study Room Reservation System Update

Effective Monday, November 6, the library will move all study room reservations from the paper sign-up sheet to the new online portal at library.greenhill.edu. The change affects the small group rooms on the second floor and the four soundproof pods on the third floor.

Reservation rules:

• Each student may book up to four hours per day across all rooms.
• Reservations open seventy-two hours in advance.
• A $10 no-show fee will be charged to any student who fails to cancel at least sixty minutes before the start time.
• Bookings for the soundproof pods require a brief orientation video, available on the portal.

Staff at the front desk will continue to assist drop-in users, and walk-in availability will be displayed on the lobby monitor in real time. For questions, please contact Ms. Vargas at the circulation desk or library@greenhill.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how far in advance can a student reserve a room?

items.text:
BIOL 215: Introduction to Marine Ecology
Instructor: Dr. Marisol Chen | Office: Halloran Science Building, Room 312
Office Hours: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM

This course meets Mondays and Wednesdays from 10:00 AM to 11:30 AM in Room 118. Students must complete all assigned readings before each session, as class discussions assume familiarity with the material.

Grading is divided as follows:
- Two midterm exams: 40% combined
- Final research project: 30%
- Weekly laboratory reports: 20%
- Participation: 10%

Laboratory reports are due every Friday by 5:00 PM through the course portal. Late reports will be accepted for up to 48 hours, but a $2.00 processing penalty is deducted from each late submission. Reports submitted after that window receive no credit.

The final research project requires a field trip to Coverly Tidal Reserve on October 18. A $15 transportation fee must be paid by October 4. Students who cannot attend should email Dr. Chen to arrange an alternative assignment.

For accommodations, please contact the Student Access Office before the second week of the semester.

The Quad Cafe — Weekly Lunch Menu
Served Monday through Friday, 11:00 AM to 2:30 PM, in the Student Union basement.

All meals include a side salad and a beverage. Prices are listed below:
- Grilled vegetable wrap: $6.50
- Roasted chicken bowl: $7.75
- Black bean chili (vegan): $5.25
- Baked salmon plate: $9.00

Daily specials rotate each weekday. On Mondays, soup of the day is $3.00 with any entree. On Wednesdays, students who show a valid library card receive a free cookie with their purchase. Friday features a $4.00 build-your-own pasta station from 12:00 PM to 1:30 PM only.

Meal-plan holders may use their card for one entree per visit at no additional charge. Guests without a plan must pay the listed prices in cash or by card. A $1.50 surcharge applies to all orders placed after 2:00 PM.

Reusable containers are available for a $5.00 refundable deposit, returned when the container is brought back clean. To reserve a large table for a group of eight or more, please email quadcafe@campus.edu at least one day in advance.

Subject: Parking Permit Renewal — Action Required by August 20

Dear Residents,

Your campus parking permit expires on August 31. To keep your assigned space in the West Lot, you must renew before August 20 through the student portal at parking.westfield.edu.

Renewal requires a $45.00 annual fee, payable by credit card or campus account. Students who renew after August 20 will be charged a $15.00 late fee and may lose their reserved space.

Please upload a current vehicle registration and your updated license plate number. The Parking Office in Carver Hall, Room 110, is open Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 4:00 PM, if you'd prefer to renew in person.

Questions? Contact us at parking@westfield.edu.

Best regards,
Ms. Dana Pruitt
Campus Parking Services

items.text:
Spring Campus Bicycle Rental Program

The Office of Campus Transportation is pleased to announce that the bicycle rental program will reopen for the spring term on March 18. Bicycles may be reserved at the Anderson Hall service desk, which is open Monday through Saturday, 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

Program details are listed below:

- A refundable deposit of $20.00 is required when a bicycle is collected.
- The standard semester pass costs $45.00 and includes a safety helmet.
- Bicycles must be returned by 9:00 PM each day to avoid a $10.00 late fee.
- Helmets are to be worn at all times while riding on campus paths.

Maintenance checks are performed every Friday, so reservations are not accepted on that day. Riders who report a mechanical problem promptly will not be charged for repairs.

For questions, contact the Office of Campus Transportation at transport@campus.edu or visit Room 112 in Anderson Hall.

Subject: Updated Details for the Geology Field Trip

Dear Students,

I am writing to confirm the new arrangements for our field trip to Granite Ridge State Park. Because of the storm last week, the trip has been moved to Saturday, October 18. We will meet outside Brennan Hall at 7:30 AM and return by 5:00 PM.

Please remember the following:
- The transportation fee of $15.00 is due at the Geology Department office by October 10.
- Bring sturdy boots, a water bottle, and a packed lunch, as no food will be sold at the site.
- Students who have not paid by the deadline cannot board the bus.

If you have any questions, please email me or stop by Room 214 during my office hours on Tuesday afternoons. I look forward to a productive day in the field.

Best regards,
Professor Lin

@maya_writes

Okay, I have to gush about last night's Open Mic at the Roastery Café — easily the best campus event I've been to all semester!! 🎤

The space only holds about forty people, so it filled up fast, but the $3 entry covered a free hot drink, which honestly made it worth it. There were poets, a jazz duo, and one guy whose stand-up had the whole room crying with laughter. The host kept things moving and even coaxed a few nervous first-timers onto the stage.

The next one is the first Thursday of December, same place, 7 p.m. If you want a spot, get there early — they don't take reservations and the line was out the door by 6:45. Bring a friend and maybe something to perform! Who's coming with me? 🙌

items.text:
Subject: Introducing the new Campus Tool Library

Dear residents,

We're excited to announce that the Campus Tool Library is now open in the basement of Hartwell Hall. Instead of buying equipment you will use only once, you can now borrow it for free with your student card.

Our shelves include power drills, hand tools, sewing machines, camping gear, and even a few musical instruments. Most items can be borrowed for up to one week. Larger tools, such as ladders and tile cutters, are limited to three days so that everyone gets a turn.

Please return items clean and on time; a $2 daily fee applies to late returns. If a tool is damaged, just let us know — honest reports help us keep everything in good working order.

Stop by Monday through Friday, 1 to 6 p.m.

Best,
The Tool Library Team

Wexler Library — Study Room Reservation Schedule
Effective Monday, October 6

Students may reserve a group study room on the third floor of Wexler Library using the library portal. Please note the following rates and rules:

- Rooms 301-305 (seats 4): free for the first two hours, then $1.00 per hour.
- Rooms 310-312 (seats 8): $2.00 per hour, with a two-hour minimum.
- The media room (Room 320): $5.00 per hour; a projector and screen are included.

Reservation hours run from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM, seven days a week. Each student may hold no more than two bookings at one time. A reserved room is held for fifteen minutes after the start time; if no one arrives, the booking is released to other students.

To cancel, sign in to the portal at least one hour ahead to avoid a $3.00 no-show charge. Questions? Email the front desk at studyrooms@wexler.edu.

items.text:
Fall Textbook Buyback — New Location
December 8 to December 12

The campus bookstore will hold its end-of-semester textbook buyback during finals week. Please note that the event has been moved from the bookstore floor to Room 118 in the Harmon Student Center, where longer counter hours can be offered.

Buyback hours are 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM each day. To sell a book, students must present a valid student ID and the book itself. Cash payment of up to $50.00 per title will be issued on the spot; titles worth more than that amount are paid by check, which is mailed within five business days.

Please note the following:
- Books with water damage or missing pages cannot be accepted.
- Lab manuals and workbooks that have been written in are not eligible.
- Editions older than the one listed for spring courses are valued at a reduced rate.

Questions about a specific title may be directed to the buyback desk at buyback@harmon.edu before December 5.

format_metadata.subject: Covering my Friday library shift

items.text:
Subject: Covering my Friday library shift

Hi Marcus,

I hope your week is going well. I'm scheduled to work the circulation desk this Friday, September 19, from 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM, but I have a dentist appointment that I couldn't move. Would you be able to take the shift for me?

If you can cover it, I'd be happy to work your Sunday morning shift next weekend in return. Either way, we both need to log the swap in the staff portal by Thursday at noon so that Ms. Okafor can approve it before payroll closes. Without her approval the hours won't be paid correctly.

Let me know by tomorrow if you can do it, and I'll enter the request right away. Thanks so much for considering it.

Thanks,
Dana

items.text:
Subject: Introducing the Campus Tool Library

Dear Residents,

We are pleased to announce that the new Tool Library has opened in the basement of Maxwell Hall, Room B12. Students living on campus can now borrow hand tools, power drills, ladders, and gardening equipment instead of buying their own. The library is open Monday through Friday, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

To borrow an item, please bring your student ID to the front desk and speak with our coordinator, Mr. Daniels. Each tool may be kept for up to seven days. A refundable deposit of $10 is required for power tools, and it will be returned when you bring the item back undamaged. There is no charge for hand tools.

If a tool is returned late, a $2 daily fee will apply until it is returned. We also welcome donations of gently used equipment, which help us expand our collection for everyone.

We hope this service makes campus life easier and more affordable. Please stop by to see what's available!

Best regards,
The Housing Office

Subject: Kitchen Faucet and Saturday Errand

Hi Marcus,

I hope your week is going well. I wanted to flag a couple of things about the apartment before the weekend. The kitchen faucet has started leaking again, and the cabinet underneath is getting damp. I called the building manager, Ms. Patel, this morning, and she said a plumber can come on Thursday between 1:00 PM and 3:00 PM. Since I have class until 4:00 PM that day, could you be home to let the plumber in?

Also, our water filter needs replacing. The hardware store on Birch Street sells the right cartridge for $15. If you can pick one up on Saturday, I'll cover the cost and pay you back. I've left a note on the fridge with the exact model number so you grab the correct one.

Thanks so much for handling this. Let me know if Thursday doesn't work for you, and I'll try to reschedule with Ms. Patel.

Best,
Elena

options.D: To pick up the filter cartridge on Birch Street

options.B: It helps Marcus buy the correct cartridge.

items.text:
NOTICE: Elevator Maintenance — Harrington Residence Hall

Scheduled service will be performed on both elevators in Harrington Hall from Monday, October 6 through Wednesday, October 8. Work will take place each day between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM. During these hours, both elevators will be taken out of operation and residents must use the north and south stairwells.

Please note the following:

- Elevator A is expected to reopen by Tuesday afternoon; Elevator B will remain closed until Wednesday evening.
- Residents who require accessibility assistance may request a temporary ground-floor room by emailing housing@campus.edu before October 3.
- Large deliveries should be rescheduled, as no freight access will be available during service.
- A $25 fee will be charged for any furniture left blocking the stairwells, which must stay clear for safety.

We apologize for the inconvenience. Questions may be directed to the Resident Manager, Mr. Alvarez, at Room 110 or by calling the front desk at extension 4400.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to Elevator B during the maintenance period?

options.A: It will reopen on Tuesday afternoon.
options.B: It will be replaced with a freight lift.
options.C: It will operate only after 5:00 PM.
options.D: It will stay closed until Wednesday evening.

questions.explanation: The bulleted list states that Elevator A is expected to reopen by Tuesday afternoon, while Elevator B will remain closed until Wednesday evening. Option A swaps the two elevators, and the others introduce conditions not stated.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a resident who uses a wheelchair?

options.A: They will be charged the $25 fee during the work.
options.B: They must move all deliveries to the stairwells.
options.C: They should contact the housing office before October 3.
options.D: They are expected to wait in the lobby each day.

questions.explanation: Since both elevators are out of service and stairwells must be used, a resident needing accessibility assistance would rely on the offer to request a ground-floor room by emailing housing@campus.edu before October 3. The other options misapply details from the notice.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a permanent closure of one stairwell.
options.B: To inform residents about a temporary elevator service.
options.C: To collect a furniture fee from every resident.
options.D: To advertise new accessibility rooms on the ground floor.

questions.explanation: The notice overall informs residents that both elevators will be serviced over three days and explains the resulting arrangements. Options A, C, and D each seize on a single detail rather than the central purpose.

items.text:
NOTICE: Holiday Hours at the Campus Mailroom

The Student Mailroom in Bradley Center will operate on a reduced schedule during the Thanksgiving break, from Wednesday, November 26 through Sunday, November 30. Regular hours will resume on Monday, December 1.

During the break, the mailroom will be open as follows:

- Wednesday, November 26: 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM only
- Thursday and Friday, November 27-28: closed
- Saturday, November 29: 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM

Packages that arrive while the mailroom is closed will be held securely and may be picked up when normal service resumes. Students must present a valid ID card to collect any parcel. A $3 storage fee will be applied to packages left uncollected for more than ten days after notification.

Please check your campus portal for pickup notices, and plan ahead if you expect a delivery during this period. For urgent questions, contact the mailroom supervisor, Ms. Park, at the Bradley Center service window.

format_metadata.title: Holiday Hours at the Campus Mailroom

options.B: From 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM.
options.C: From 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM.
options.D: From 12:00 PM to 5:00 PM.

questions.explanation: The schedule lists Saturday, November 29 as 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM. Option A uses the Wednesday hours, while B and D give times that appear nowhere in the notice.

options.A: Pay a $3 fee at every pickup.
options.B: Present a valid ID card.
options.C: Email the mailroom supervisor first.
options.D: Arrive before Wednesday, November 26.

questions.explanation: The notice states that students must present a valid ID card to collect any parcel. The $3 fee applies only after ten days, contacting the supervisor is for urgent questions, and no arrival deadline is required for pickup.

options.A: It will be held until service resumes.

options.C: It will incur a storage fee the same day.
options.D: It will be delivered to the student's room.

questions.explanation: The mailroom is closed on Thursday, November 27, and the notice says packages arriving while it is closed are held securely for later pickup. The other options contradict the stated handling of held packages.

items.text:
Subject: Work-Study Award — Action Needed by September 5

Dear Ms. Reyes,

Your campus work-study award for the fall semester has been approved. To activate it, please complete two steps before September 5.

First, log in to the Student Finance Portal and accept the $2,400 award. Second, visit the Bursar's Office in Hale Hall, Room 112, to submit a signed tax form and a valid photo ID.

Awards not accepted by the deadline are released to other applicants. If you have already found an off-campus job, you may decline the award online instead.

For questions, email workstudy@campus.edu or stop by during walk-in hours, Monday through Thursday, 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM.

Best regards,
Daniel Okafor
Financial Aid Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Work-Study Award — Action Needed by September 5

questions.explanation: The email states she must 'submit a signed tax form and a valid photo ID' at the Bursar's Office, which matches option B.

questions.explanation: The email's central aim is to tell the student the two steps required to activate an already-approved work-study award; the job, tuition, and hours are not the focus.

questions.explanation: The email says 'Awards not accepted by the deadline are released to other applicants,' so a student who misses the deadline risks losing the award to someone else.
